Published A Method of Reaching Extreme Altitudes in 1919. The New York Times mocked me -- said rockets cannot work in a vacuum. Newton Third Law. On March 16, 1926, I launched the first liquid-fueled rocket. It flew for 2.5 seconds, reached 41 feet. Pathetic by any standard except it was the first time in history. Tip: The press will mock you. The military will ignore you. Do the work anyway. Document everything. Von Braun admitted his V-2s used my patents. NASA Goddard Space Flight Center is named for me. I never saw it.

users.tagline: Every day sees humanity more victorious in the struggle with space and time
users.bio: Born in Bologna, Italy. My mother was Annie Jameson, of the Jameson Irish whiskey family. I studied under Augusto Righi at Bologna, who worked on Hertz electromagnetic waves. At 20, I began experiments in my father attic. In 1901, I received the first transatlantic radio signal -- the letter S, three dots -- from Cornwall to Newfoundland, 3,500 km. The ionosphere bounced the waves. I got lucky. I also got the Nobel Prize. Tip: Hertz discovered radio waves. I made them useful. Tesla built better transmitters. I built the business. The inventor and the entrepreneur are different skills. You need both. My wireless saved 700 lives on the Titanic.

users.tagline: Whether you think you can, or you think you cannot -- you are right
users.bio: Born in Dearborn, Michigan, on a farm. Hated farming. Loved machines. At 12, I saw a steam engine on the road and never looked back. Built my first gasoline engine on the kitchen table in 1893 -- my wife Clara held it steady while I tested it. My first two car companies failed. The third succeeded because of one idea: the moving assembly line. Model T build time dropped from 12 hours to 93 minutes. Price dropped from $850 to $260. I paid workers $5 a day -- double the rate. Workers who can afford the product they build become customers. Tip: Simplify. Model T came in one color -- black -- because black paint dried fastest. Edison was my mentor and friend. He told me to keep going.

users.tagline: I got my start by giving myself a start
users.bio: Born Sarah Breedlove in Delta, Louisiana. First person in my family born free. Orphaned at 7, married at 14, widowed at 20 with a daughter. Worked as a washerwoman for $1.50 a day. Began losing my hair from stress, poor diet, and harsh products. Developed my own formula -- the Walker System -- for scalp treatment and hair growth. Built a company, a factory, trained thousands of Walker Agents -- Black women earning their own money when few doors were open. First self-made female millionaire in America. Tip: I did not start with money. I started with a problem I understood personally. Every great business starts with personal pain that becomes universal solution. Also: pay your people well and they become your best advertisement. Booker T. Washington thought I was too flashy. I told him I came from the cotton fields of the South and built this with my own hands.

users.tagline: I never in a thousand years expected that small laboratory project would unfold as it did
users.bio: Born in New Kensington, Pennsylvania. My father died when I was 10. He was a naturalist who took me on walks through the woods to study plants. My mother was a seamstress who taught me to sew. I wanted to be a doctor but could not afford medical school, so I took a temporary job at DuPont to save money. I stayed 40 years. In 1965, I was working on lightweight fibers for tires when I produced a strange, cloudy solution. Most chemists would have thrown it away. I insisted on spinning it. The fiber was five times stronger than steel by weight. Kevlar. It now protects police officers, soldiers, firefighters, and astronauts. Tip: When the experiment gives you something unexpected, do not discard it. Investigate it. The biggest discoveries come from anomalies, not from predictions. Also: temporary jobs sometimes become life work.

users.tagline: Science and everyday life cannot and should not be separated
users.bio: Born in Notting Hill, London, into a prominent Anglo-Jewish family. Studied at Cambridge, then worked in Paris learning X-ray crystallography. Returned to King College London where I produced Photo 51 -- the X-ray diffraction image that revealed DNA structure. Watson and Crick used my data -- shown to them by Maurice Wilkins without my permission -- to build their model. They got the Nobel Prize. I was never told they had seen my work. I died of ovarian cancer at 37, likely from years of X-ray exposure. Tip: Document your work meticulously and guard your data. The history of science is full of stolen credit. Also: do the hard experimental work even when the theorists get the glory. Without Photo 51, Watson and Crick had nothing but speculation. The evidence is everything.

users.tagline: In every outward and visible grace of life, in every truth of nature, there is a mystery
users.bio: Born in Springdale, Pennsylvania. My mother taught me to love nature on walks through the woods. Started as a marine biologist for the US Fish and Wildlife Service. Published The Sea Around Us in 1951 -- a bestseller that made ocean science accessible. Then in 1962, Silent Spring. I documented how DDT and other pesticides were poisoning birds, fish, and the entire food chain. The chemical industry attacked me viciously -- called me hysterical, a communist, an unmarried woman who had no business in science. I had breast cancer and knew I was dying. I testified before Congress anyway. DDT was banned. The EPA was created. The modern environmental movement began. Tip: Write clearly enough that non-scientists understand, and precisely enough that scientists cannot dismiss you. The pen is more powerful than the spray plane. My friend Dorothy Freeman and I exchanged hundreds of letters. Find someone who believes in your work when the world attacks.

users.tagline: My dynamite will sooner lead to peace than a thousand world conventions
users.bio: Born in Stockholm, Sweden. My father was an engineer and inventor who went bankrupt, moved us to St. Petersburg, and rebuilt. I was privately tutored, spoke five languages by 17. Studied chemistry in Paris under Theophile-Jules Pelouze. My brother Emil was killed in a nitroglycerin explosion at our factory in 1864. That drove me to tame the substance. Mixed nitroglycerin with diatomaceous earth: dynamite. Stable, safe to handle, devastating when detonated. Then detonating caps. Then blasting gelatin. Then ballistite. 355 patents. Factories in 20 countries. Then I read my own obituary -- a French newspaper confused me with my brother Ludvig and published it. The headline: The Merchant of Death Is Dead. I decided my fortune should fund prizes for peace, literature, and science. Tip: What the world says about you after you die matters. You still have time to change the story. My friend Bertha von Suttner convinced me the peace prize was essential.

users.tagline: The special theory of relativity owes its origins to Maxwell's equations of the electromagnetic field -- Albert Einstein
users.bio: Born in Edinburgh, Scotland. Published my first scientific paper at 14 -- on oval curves. At Cambridge, I unified electricity, magnetism, and light into four equations. Maxwell equations. Everything from radio waves to X-rays to visible light to microwaves -- all the same phenomenon, differing only in wavelength. I predicted electromagnetic waves travel at the speed of light. Therefore light IS an electromagnetic wave. Hertz proved it experimentally eight years after my death. Einstein kept a photograph of me on his wall alongside Newton and Faraday. Tip: If you can express a physical law as a mathematical equation, you can predict phenomena no one has ever observed. I predicted radio waves with mathematics alone. The equations do not care about human doubt. Also pioneered statistical mechanics and produced the first color photograph in 1861. Faraday gave me the experimental foundation. I gave the world the mathematical framework.

users.tagline: I saw in a dream a table where all the elements fell into place as required
users.bio: Born in Tobolsk, Siberia, the youngest of 14 children. My mother walked me 1,500 miles to Moscow so I could attend university -- then to St. Petersburg when Moscow rejected me. She died weeks after I was admitted. In 1869, I arranged the 63 known elements by atomic weight and valence. I noticed a pattern -- elements with similar properties appeared at regular intervals. The periodic table. I left gaps for elements not yet discovered and predicted their properties. Gallium, germanium, scandium -- all found within 15 years, matching my predictions exactly. Tip: The table came to me in a dream, but only because I had been studying element properties obsessively for months. You dream about what you work on. Feed your mind and your subconscious will solve the puzzle. Also: I was denied the Nobel Prize by one vote. Sometimes the world is slow to recognize what matters.

users.tagline: Access to communication in the widest sense is access to knowledge
users.bio: Born in Coupvray, France. My father was a leatherworker. At age three, I was playing with his tools and an awl slipped into my eye. The infection spread to both eyes. Blind by five. Attended the Royal Institute for Blind Youth in Paris at 10. At 15, I adapted Charles Barbier night writing system into a six-dot cell that could represent any letter, number, or musical note. The Institute resisted my system for decades. The sighted administrators could not read it and felt threatened. It was adopted widely only after my death. Tip: The people who benefit most from your invention are not always the people who decide whether to adopt it. Persistence outlasts resistance. Six dots. Sixty-three combinations. Enough for any language on Earth.

users.tagline: I visualized the entire television system while plowing a potato field at age 14
users.bio: Born in Beaver, Utah, in a log cabin with no electricity. When we moved to Rigby, Idaho, I found science magazines in the attic. At 14, plowing a potato field, I watched the rows of turned earth and imagined scanning an image line by line with an electron beam. That vision became television. Built the first fully electronic TV at 21 in San Francisco. RCA and David Sarnoff fought me for years. I won in court but lost in life -- they had the money and manufacturing. My high school teacher Justin Tolman testified that I had drawn the design on his blackboard in 1922. That drawing won my patent case. Tip: Document your ideas obsessively. A sketch on a blackboard, witnessed by your teacher, won a Supreme Court patent case.

users.tagline: One of my primary objects is to form the tools so the tools themselves shall fashion the work
users.bio: Born in Westborough, Massachusetts. At 14, I was manufacturing nails in my father's workshop. Graduated Yale at 27. On a Georgia plantation, I saw the labor of separating cotton seeds from fiber. Built the cotton gin in 10 days. Everyone copied it. Patents were unenforceable. The gin also tragically increased demand for slave labor. Later, I pioneered interchangeable parts for muskets -- any trigger fits any musket. Mass production before Ford. Tip: A simple invention is easy to copy. Protect with manufacturing advantage, not just patents. Also: consider second-order effects. The cotton gin made slavery more profitable. Intentions do not control consequences.

users.tagline: There is grandeur in this view of life
users.bio: Born in Shrewsbury, England. My father was a wealthy physician who thought I was destined for nothing. Dropped out of medical school because I could not stand surgery without anesthesia. Then sailed on the Beagle for five years. The Galapagos finches, the fossil record -- it all pointed to natural selection. I sat on the theory for 20 years, terrified. When Alfred Russel Wallace sent me a paper with the same idea, I finally published. On the Origin of Species, 1859. Tip: I spent eight years studying barnacles. Those barnacles taught me more about variation than any textbook. My friends Joseph Hooker and Thomas Huxley defended my work when I could not face the debates.

users.tagline: I attribute my success to this: I never gave or took any excuse
users.bio: Born in Florence, Italy, to wealthy English parents -- hence the name. Felt a calling to nursing at 16. My family was horrified. In 1854, I took 38 nurses to the Crimean War. Scutari hospital was a death trap. I reduced the death rate from 42 percent to 2 percent through sanitation. Then changed the world with data. My polar area diagrams showed Parliament visually that sanitation saves lives. Tip: You cannot argue with a picture. Data presented visually is more persuasive than any speech. The real enemy is not disease -- it is bureaucratic indifference. My friend Sidney Herbert helped me get access to the army.

users.tagline: What we observe is not nature itself, but nature exposed to our method of questioning
users.bio: Born in Wuerzburg, Germany. My father was a professor of medieval Greek. At 23, I formulated matrix mechanics -- the first complete framework for quantum theory. At 25, the uncertainty principle: you cannot simultaneously know both exact position and exact momentum of a particle. This is not instrument limitation -- it is a fundamental property of nature. Won the Nobel at 31. Tip: The uncertainty principle applies beyond physics. In business, relationships, self-knowledge -- observing changes what is observed. Precision in one measurement costs precision in another. My teacher Niels Bohr became my greatest intellectual opponent. Argue with your mentors.

users.tagline: Since the antiseptic treatment has been brought into full operation, my wards have completely changed their character
users.bio: Born in Upton, Essex, England. My father was a wine merchant and amateur microscopist who improved the achromatic lens. Became professor of surgery at Glasgow. Surgical mortality from infection ran 45 to 50 percent. Surgeons operated in street clothes with unwashed hands. I read Pasteur on germ theory and started using carbolic acid to sterilize wounds, instruments, and dressings. Mortality dropped to 15 percent. Surgeons fought me for years -- the suggestion their hands were dirty was an insult. Tip: When people resist your evidence, it is because accepting it means admitting they have been doing harm. Be persistent. Let the mortality statistics speak. Pasteur gave me the theory. Nightingale and I approached the same problem from different angles.

items.description: Single-axle cargo trailer, 750kg capacity, no special license needed. 2.5m x 1.3m bed with fold-down sides. Includes ratchet straps, wheel chocks, and trailer lock. Tow ball is standard 50mm. I'll check your car's tow rating before I hand over the keys -- safety first.

media.alt_text: Cargo utility trailer with fold-down sides

listings.notes: Return clean. Mud = EUR 30 cleaning fee. Weekend rate EUR 50 (Fri evening to Mon morning). Insurance your responsibility.

items.name: Electric Cargo Bike -- Babboe City Mountain
items.description: Front-loading electric cargo bike with rain cover. Carries up to 100kg or 2 kids + groceries. Shimano mid-drive motor, 80km range. Perfect for market runs, beach gear, or kid transport in the centro storico where cars can't go. Includes lock, helmet, and rain cover.

media.alt_text: Electric cargo bike with front loading box

listings.notes: Full charge on pickup. Charger included for multi-day. Helmet mandatory. ID required. Weekly rate EUR 120.

items.name: Appliance Repair Service -- Washers, Dryers, Dishwashers
items.description: I fix washing machines, dryers, dishwashers, and ovens. 15 years experience with all major brands (Bosch, Samsung, LG, Whirlpool, Indesit). Diagnostic visit EUR 40 -- fully refunded if you hire me for the repair. Most repairs same-day. I bring my tools, you just need to be home.

media.alt_text: Technician repairing washing machine

listings.notes: EUR 40 diagnostic visit -- refunded if you hire me. Labor EUR 35/hour after diagnosis. Parts at cost + 10%. Most repairs EUR 80-150 total. No fix, no charge beyond diagnostic.

items.name: Restauro mobili antichi -- falegname esperto
items.description: Restauro professionale di mobili antichi e d'epoca. Riparazione strutturale, sostituzione impiallacciatura, lucidatura a gommalacca, trattamento tarli. 40 anni di esperienza. Mandate foto per un preventivo gratuito via WhatsApp. Per pezzi complessi, visita a domicilio EUR 50 (scalati dal lavoro).

media.alt_text: Craftsman restoring antique wooden furniture

listings.notes: Visita a domicilio EUR 50 (scalati se accettate il lavoro). Preventivo gratuito via foto WhatsApp. Ritiro e consegna disponibili entro Trapani (EUR 30). Tempi: 1-4 settimane a seconda del lavoro.
Restauri complessi: prezzo da concordare dopo la visita. Mandate foto del mobile + dettagli del danno.

items.name: Bike Tune-Up & Repair Service
items.description: Full bike tune-up: brakes, gears, chain, tires, cables, bearings. I work on road bikes, mountain bikes, city bikes, and e-bikes (non-motor parts). 10 years as a weekend mechanic. Drop off at my shed or I come to you (within Trapani). Most tune-ups done in 1-2 hours while you wait.

media.alt_text: Bike mechanic adjusting gears on a bicycle

listings.notes: Basic tune-up EUR 30 (brakes, gears, chain lube, tire pressure). Full overhaul EUR 60 (+ bearings, cables, deep clean). Parts extra at cost. Mobile service +EUR 10.

items.name: Plumbing Repair -- Leaks, Taps, Drains
items.description: Basic plumbing repairs: leaky taps, toilet flush mechanisms, blocked drains, pipe joints, washing machine connections. NOT a licensed plumber -- I'm a garage guy who's fixed his own house for 30 years. Simple stuff I can handle. Complex jobs I'll tell you straight: call a pro.

media.alt_text: Plumber fixing sink pipe under cabinet

listings.notes: Minimum 1 hour (EUR 25). Most tap/drain fixes done in under an hour. Parts at cost -- I'll show you the receipt. If it's beyond my skill, I tell you and you pay nothing. Honest work.

items.name: Riparazione impianto irrigazione giardino
items.description: Riparo e installo impianti di irrigazione a goccia e a sprinkler. Tubi rotti, gocciolatori intasati, programmatori, elettrovalvole. 30 anni di esperienza con giardini e orti. Mandate foto del problema e vi dico subito se posso aiutarvi. Lavoro solo su impianti esterni, non idraulica di casa.

media.alt_text: Garden irrigation system with drip lines

listings.notes: Minimo 2 ore (EUR 60). Materiali a parte. Sopralluogo gratuito se il lavoro viene fatto. Altrimenti EUR 20 per il viaggio.

items.name: Corso di saldatura base -- MIG e arco
items.description: Impara a saldare da zero. 35 anni di esperienza professionale. Il corso copre: sicurezza, preparazione materiale, saldatura MIG, saldatura ad arco, giunti di base. Tutto il materiale e le attrezzature incluse. Alla fine costruisci il tuo primo progetto da portare a casa.

media.alt_text: Welding training with sparks flying in workshop

listings.notes: Sessione di 4 ore. Massimo 2 studenti per sessione. DPI inclusi (maschera, guanti, grembiule). Portate solo scarpe chiuse. Sabato mattina o su appuntamento.

items.name: Photography Walk & Lesson -- Trapani Old Town
items.description: Learn photography while exploring Trapani's stunning old town. Composition, lighting, street photography, golden hour techniques. Bring your camera (phone is fine too). I'll teach you to see light the way artists do. We end at a cafe overlooking the sea. Small groups only -- 3 people max.

media.alt_text: Photographer teaching during golden hour in old town

listings.notes: 2.5 hours. Sunset walk preferred (16:30 start, winter) or morning (7:30, summer). Coffee at the end included. Rain date guaranteed -- Sicily rain doesn't last long.

items.name: Lezione di cucina siciliana -- pasta fresca e dolci
items.description: Vi insegno a fare la pasta fresca (busiate, cavatelli, ravioli) e i dolci siciliani (cannoli, cassata, frutta di Martorana). Ricette della nonna, niente scorciatoie. Cuciniamo insieme e poi mangiamo tutto. La mia cucina, i miei ingredienti, la vostra fame.

media.alt_text: Making fresh pasta by hand in Sicilian kitchen

listings.notes: 3 ore, pranzo incluso (mangiamo quello che cuciniamo). Massimo 4 persone. Ingredienti inclusi. Portate il grembiule o ve ne presto uno. Mercoledì e venerdì pomeriggio.

items.name: Electronics & Soldering Workshop for Beginners
items.description: Learn to solder, read schematics, and build your first circuit. We'll start with basic theory (15 min), then hands-on: through-hole soldering, desoldering, component identification. You leave with a working LED blinker circuit you built yourself. All materials included.

media.alt_text: Soldering iron on circuit board in electronics workshop

listings.notes: 2 hours. All tools and components included. Ages 12+ welcome (minors with parent). Max 3 students. Saturday afternoons at my bench.

items.name: Corso cura olivi e potatura -- 50 anni di esperienza
items.description: Vi insegno tutto sugli ulivi: potatura di formazione, potatura di produzione, trattamento della mosca olearia, raccolta, e spremitura. Lavoriamo nel mio oliveto (12 alberi). Teoria la mattina, pratica il pomeriggio. Portate guanti da lavoro e scarpe robuste.

media.alt_text: Olive trees in Mediterranean garden

listings.notes: Giornata intera (9:00-16:00). Pranzo incluso (cucino io). Massimo 4 persone. Stagione potatura: novembre-febbraio. Stagione raccolta: ottobre-novembre.

items.name: Mobili su misura in legno massello
items.description: Costruisco mobili su misura: librerie, tavoli, armadi, porte, scale. Solo legno massello (castagno, noce, frassino, ulivo). Niente truciolato, niente IKEA. Ogni pezzo è unico. Mandate misure e foto dello spazio, vi mando un preventivo dettagliato entro 48 ore.

media.alt_text: Custom handmade wooden bookshelf in workshop

listings.notes: Preventivo gratuito via foto/misure. Visita a domicilio EUR 50 (scalati dal lavoro se accettate). Tempi: 2-6 settimane. Acconto 40% alla conferma, saldo alla consegna. Garanzia 5 anni sulla struttura.

items.name: Commissioned Portrait Painting -- Oil on Canvas
items.description: Custom oil portrait from your photo. Realistic or impressionist style. Canvas sizes from 30x40cm to 100x80cm. I work from high-resolution photos -- send 3-5 from different angles. Each portrait takes 2-4 weeks. Perfect gift for anniversaries, memorials, or just because your dog deserves it.

media.alt_text: Artist painting portrait on canvas in studio

listings.notes: Starting at EUR 150 for 30x40cm. 50x70cm = EUR 280. 100x80cm = EUR 450. 50% deposit upfront. Revision round included. Shipping available (EU only). Dogs and cats cost the same as humans.

items.name: Custom 3D Printed Parts & Prototypes
items.description: Send me your STL file or describe what you need, and I'll print it. PLA, PETG, or TPU materials. Max build volume 220x220x250mm. Perfect for replacement parts, enclosures, brackets, prototypes, or that weird adapter nobody sells. I can also design the part if you send dimensions and sketches.

media.alt_text: 3D printer creating custom part

listings.notes: Starting EUR 5 for simple parts. Price based on material + print time. Design service EUR 15/hour if you need me to model it. Rush jobs (24h) +50%. Send STL for instant quote.

items.name: Progettazione giardino su misura
items.description: Disegno il vostro giardino dei sogni: piante mediterranee, irrigazione efficiente, spazi per mangiare all'aperto, orto produttivo. Lavoro con quello che avete -- non serve ricominciare da zero. Prima visita per capire il terreno, l'esposizione, e i vostri desideri. Poi un progetto su carta con lista piante e costi.

media.alt_text: Mediterranean garden design with stone paths and herbs

listings.notes: Visita + progetto completo EUR 80 (giardini fino a 100mq). Giardini grandi: da concordare. Realizzazione del giardino: preventivo separato dopo il progetto. Posso anche solo consigliarvi le piante giuste per EUR 30 (1 ora al telefono con foto).
Consulenza telefonica con foto: EUR 30/ora. Vi dico cosa piantare, quando, e come curarlo. Niente visite, solo consigli pratici.

items.name: Custom Sicilian Pastries for Events
items.description: Handmade Sicilian pastries for your event: cannoli (ricotta or pistachio), cassatelle, iris, frutta di Martorana, buccellato. Minimum order 20 pieces. Made fresh the morning of your event. Everything from scratch, no shortcuts -- real sheep's milk ricotta, hand-rolled shells, Bronte pistachios.

media.alt_text: Assortment of Sicilian cannoli and pastries on display

listings.notes: EUR 3.50 per piece (cannoli, cassatelle). Minimum 20 pieces = EUR 70. Frutta di Martorana EUR 5/piece. Order 3 days in advance. Pickup from my kitchen or delivery within Trapani EUR 10. Large orders (100+) = 10% discount.

items.name: Garden Maintenance & Cleanup -- The Full Day Crew
items.description: Two guys, shovels, wheelbarrow, hedge trimmer, leaf blower. We show up, we work, we clean up. Garden cleanup, hedge trimming, weed removal, basic landscaping, green waste hauling. No fancy quotes -- minimum EUR 200 covers 1 hour travel + 3 hours work for 2 workers. Need us longer? EUR 30/hour per worker after that. We bring the tools, you point at what needs doing.

media.alt_text: Garden workers with wheelbarrow doing yard cleanup

listings.notes: MINIMUM EUR 200 = 2 workers, 1hr travel + 3hrs work. Extra hours EUR 30/worker/hr. Green waste removal EUR 40/trailer load. We quote via photos first -- send pics of your garden via WhatsApp, we tell you how long it'll take. Consultation visit EUR 50 (refunded if you hire us). Don't waste our time and we won't waste yours.

items.name: Interior Painting Service -- Rooms & Apartments
items.description: Interior painting: walls, ceilings, doors, window frames. Prep work included (filling holes, sanding, masking). I bring drop cloths, rollers, brushes. You buy the paint (I'll tell you exactly what and how much). Clean lines, no drips, furniture moved and protected. 30 years of doing my own house -- time to do yours.

media.alt_text: Person painting interior wall with roller

listings.notes: EUR 150 per standard room (up to 15 sqm walls). Ceiling +EUR 50. Multiple rooms: 10% off after 3rd room. Full apartment quote via photos. Minimum job EUR 150. Paint NOT included -- I'll send you the shopping list. Weekend jobs +20%.

items.name: Servizio traslochi -- squadra da 2-3 persone
items.description: Traslochi locali a Trapani e provincia. Squadra di 2-3 persone con furgone, cinghie, coperte, carrello. Smontiamo mobili, li carichiamo, li trasportiamo, li rimontiamo. 10 anni di esperienza -- sappiamo come portare un armadio per le scale strette del centro storico senza graffiare niente.

media.alt_text: Moving team carrying furniture into van

listings.notes: MINIMO EUR 250 = 2 uomini + furgone, 4 ore (carico + trasporto + scarico). Terzo uomo +EUR 25/ora. Oltre le 4 ore: EUR 35/ora per uomo. Smontaggio/rimontaggio mobili incluso. Scala senza ascensore: +EUR 10/piano. Preventivo gratuito via foto degli ambienti.

items.name: Deep Cleaning Service -- Move-In/Move-Out
items.description: Professional deep cleaning for apartments and houses. Move-in, move-out, post-renovation, or just because it's been too long. Every surface: kitchen (oven, fridge inside), bathroom (grout, limescale), floors (mop + wax), windows, baseboards. I bring all products and equipment. You come back to a place that smells like new.

media.alt_text: Professional deep cleaning service in modern apartment
Kitchen deep cleaning -- sparkling countertops
Bathroom deep cleaning -- tiles and grout

listings.notes: EUR 120 for apartment up to 60 sqm. EUR 180 up to 100 sqm. EUR 250 for full house. Products included. Windows +EUR 5 each. Post-renovation cleanup +30% (dust is evil). Keys or be home -- I need 4-6 hours uninterrupted.

items.name: Pressure Washing Service -- Patios, Driveways, Walls
items.description: High-pressure washing for patios, driveways, garden walls, pool surrounds, and exterior surfaces. Karcher K5 professional with surface cleaner attachment. Removes years of dirt, moss, algae, and black marks. The before-and-after will blow your mind. I bring the machine, you provide the water tap.

media.alt_text: Pressure washing stone patio clean
Power washing concrete driveway
Pressure washing exterior wall -- moss removal

listings.notes: EUR 3 per square meter. Minimum job EUR 60 (20 sqm). Driveway average EUR 90-150. Send photos for exact quote. I need access to a water tap and power outlet. Best results on stone, concrete, tile. Not suitable for wood decking.

items.name: Handyman by the Hour -- General Home Repairs
items.description: Jack of all trades, master of getting things done. Shelf mounting, door repair, lock changes, curtain rails, TV wall mounts, furniture assembly, small electrical (light switches, outlets), minor plumbing. If it takes less than a day and doesn't need a licensed specialist, I can probably fix it.

media.alt_text: Handyman with toolbelt fixing shelves on wall

listings.notes: Minimum 2 hours (EUR 50). I bring basic tools. Specialized parts/materials at cost. List your jobs in advance so I bring the right tools -- one trip, multiple fixes, best value. Weekends: EUR 30/hour.

items.name: Kayak Tandem -- Explore the Coast
items.description: Sit-on-top tandem kayak (2 person). Stable, easy to paddle, great for exploring the coast between Trapani and San Vito. Includes 2 paddles, 2 life vests, dry bag, and a basic map of good spots. I'll meet you at the beach and show you how to launch.

media.alt_text: Tandem kayak on clear Mediterranean water

listings.notes: Full day rental. Pickup/return at Lido beach. Weekly rate EUR 150. I'll deliver to other beaches within 20km for EUR 15. Life vests mandatory -- non-negotiable.

items.name: La Marzocco Linea Mini (Espresso Machine)
items.description: The La Marzocco Linea Mini. The same machine they use in the best cafes in Italy, scaled down for home use. Dual boiler, PID temperature control, pre-infusion. I have two -- one at the villa, one on the yacht. This one is from the villa. Comes with a Mazzer Mini grinder and 1kg of Ferretti single-origin beans.

media.alt_text: La Marzocco Linea Mini espresso machine on marble counter

listings.notes: Pickup from Villa Oleandra, Trapani. I'll give you a 15-minute lesson on how to pull a proper shot. Grinder included. Beans NOT included after the first kilo.

items.story: I bought this machine after a barista in Milan told me my espresso was 'acceptable.' Acceptable. I spent EUR 4,200 to never hear that word again.

items.name: Nespresso Citiz (Backup Machine)
items.description: My backup Nespresso Citiz. For when the Linea Mini is rented out or when I can't be bothered to grind beans. Makes a decent espresso in 25 seconds. Pod machine -- not for purists, but perfect for mortals. Comes with a starter pack of 50 capsules.

media.alt_text: Nespresso Citiz espresso machine

listings.notes: Free delivery within Trapani. 50 capsules included. After that, buy your own. I recommend the Roma blend.

items.name: Vespa Primavera 125 (Vintage, 1978)
items.description: Original 1978 Vespa Primavera 125. Celeste blue. Fully restored. Rides like a dream through the narrow streets of Trapani. Helmet, lock, and a hand-drawn map of the best gelato stops included. You scratch it, you buy it. Insurance required.

media.alt_text: Vintage celeste blue Vespa Primavera 125

listings.notes: Valid license required. Must be 18+. Weekly rate EUR 200. Insurance certificate needed before I hand over the key. This is not a toy.
Thinking of selling. If the right offer comes along, she goes to a good home.

items.story: I found this Vespa in a barn outside Marsala in 2019. The farmer wanted EUR 500 for it. I gave him EUR 800 because it was worth more than he knew. Took 6 months to restore. She sounds like a sewing machine and smells like history.

items.name: Yacht Day Trip -- Trapani to Favignana
items.description: A full day on my 42-foot sailing yacht. Trapani marina to Favignana island and back. Swim stops, lunch on board, sunset return. I captain. You relax. Maximum 6 guests. Bring sunscreen and a good attitude.

media.alt_text: Sailing yacht on turquoise Mediterranean water near Favignana

listings.notes: Full day trip 9AM-7PM. Lunch, drinks, and snorkeling gear included. No experience needed. Minimum 2 guests. Cancellation if wind exceeds 25 knots.

items.story: I bought the boat because someone told me actors shouldn't know how to sail. I now have a captain's license and more sea miles than most fishermen in Trapani. The bilge pump works. Usually.

items.name: PlayStation 5 (Brand New, Sealed)
items.description: Brand new PS5, still in box. My cousin got two for Christmas and doesn't need this one. Rent it for a weekend or buy it outright. Best price in Trapani. No receipt but it's legit, trust me.

media.alt_text: PlayStation 5 console box

listings.notes: Fast delivery anywhere in Trapani. Cash preferred. No questions asked.

items.name: iPhone 14 Pro Max (Like New)
items.description: iPhone 14 Pro Max, 256GB, space black. Like new. My friend upgraded to the 16 and gave me this. Screen perfect, battery 94%. Great for temporary use or if your phone broke. Weekly rental only.

media.alt_text: iPhone 14 Pro Max space black

listings.notes: Weekly rental EUR 80. Must leave ID as deposit. Cash only.

items.name: Pottery Wheel Session -- Make Your Own
items.description: Hands-on pottery session on a professional electric wheel. I'll teach you centering, pulling, and shaping. You make 2-3 pieces (bowl, cup, vase) and I fire them in my kiln. Pick them up glazed and finished 2 weeks later. Great date activity, great rainy day activity, great any day activity.

media.alt_text: Hands shaping clay on pottery wheel

listings.notes: 2-hour session. Clay, glazing, and firing included. Max 2 people per session. Finished pieces ready in 2 weeks. Couples session EUR 70 (2 people). Wear clothes you don't mind getting dirty.

items.name: Garden Hose (30m, old but works)
items.description: 30-meter garden hose. Been sitting in the garage for 2 years. Still holds water, no leaks. Free to whoever picks it up. I upgraded to a retractable one.

listings.notes: Pick up from garage anytime. Just message me first.

items.story: Bought it when we moved in. Used it exactly twice. The retractable one changed my life.

media.alt_text: Woman watering plants with garden hose in yard
Person spraying water with garden hose
Watering garden with hose - close up

items.name: Bread Maker (Panasonic SD-2500)
items.description: Panasonic bread maker. Makes great bread but I went back to doing it by hand. Works perfectly, just collecting dust. Comes with manual and measuring cup.

listings.notes: Free! Nothing beats hand-kneaded bread. This machine deserves someone who'll use it.

items.story: I got seduced by the convenience. Then I remembered why I became a baker -- the feeling of dough under your hands. Can't automate that.

media.alt_text: Fresh baked bread from bread maker
Bread loaves fresh from oven
Homemade bread on cutting board

items.name: Kids' Bicycle (16 inch, pink)
items.description: Girls' bicycle, 16 inch wheels. My granddaughter outgrew it. Training wheels included. Some scratches but rides perfectly. Free to a good home.

listings.notes: Free! Comes with training wheels and a bell. Perfect for ages 4-6.

items.story: Sofia learned to ride on this bike. Every scratch is a story. Now she wants a big-girl bike. Time to pass this one on.

media.alt_text: Kids bicycle with training wheels
Child's bicycle outdoors
Kids bike parked on path

items.name: Canon EOS R5 Camera Kit
items.description: Canon EOS R5 mirrorless camera body with 24-70mm f/2.8L lens, 70-200mm f/2.8L lens, 2 batteries, charger, and camera bag. Professional grade, perfect for events and commercial shoots.

media.alt_text: Canon EOS R5 camera body - front view
Canon camera with lens kit - complete set
Professional camera in use - photography session

listings.notes: Includes 30-min orientation. Insurance required for events. SD cards not included.

items.story: Saved for 2 years to buy this kit. It's my workhorse but sits idle 3 days a week. Rather than let it sleep in the bag, I rent it to photographers who need pro gear without the pro price tag.

items.name: Photography Studio Backdrop Kit
items.description: Complete backdrop system: 3m x 6m support stand, 3 muslin backdrops (white, black, grey), 2 softbox lights, reflector kit. Sets up in 15 minutes.

media.alt_text: Photography backdrop kit - studio setup
Studio lighting softbox equipment

listings.notes: Free delivery within Trapani. Setup assistance available for EUR 10.

items.name: Electrical Testing Kit (Fluke)
items.description: Fluke 117 multimeter, Fluke T6 voltage tester, wire stripper set, crimping tool, cable puller, voltage detector pen. Everything you need for residential electrical work.

media.alt_text: Electrical testing kit with Fluke multimeter
Wire strippers and electrical tools laid out
Fluke multimeter close-up display

listings.notes: I'll show you how to use the Fluke if you've never used one. 5-minute crash course included.

items.story: A good multimeter costs EUR 300+. Most people need one for a single job. Rent mine, save EUR 280, do the job right.

items.name: Honda EU2200i Portable Generator
items.description: Honda EU2200i inverter generator. 2200W, super quiet (48-57 dB), fuel efficient (8hrs on 1 tank). Perfect for camping, outdoor events, or backup power. Includes fuel can and oil.

media.alt_text: Honda portable generator - compact unit
Generator in use at outdoor event

listings.notes: Delivered with full tank. Return with full tank or pay EUR 10 refueling fee. Free delivery within 10km.

items.name: Wüsthof Professional Knife Set (8 pieces)
items.description: Wüsthof Classic 8-piece knife set in walnut block. Chef's knife, bread knife, utility, paring, carving, santoku, kitchen shears, honing steel. German forged, razor sharp.

media.alt_text: Wüsthof knife set in walnut block
Professional chef's knife on cutting board
Chef preparing food with professional knife

items.name: Commercial Pasta Machine (Imperia)
items.description: Imperia RMN220 electric pasta machine with 6 attachments -- spaghetti, fettuccine, ravioli, lasagna, tagliatelle, and pappardelle. Motor-driven, table clamp mount. Makes 15kg/hour.

media.alt_text: Commercial pasta machine with attachments
Fresh pasta being made with machine

2-hour pasta making class. You go home with 1kg of fresh pasta and the skills to make more. All ingredients included.

items.name: Pipe Threading Set (RIDGID)
items.description: RIDGID 12-R manual pipe threader with 6 die heads (1/2" to 2"), pipe cutter, reamer, pipe vise, thread sealant, and carrying case. Everything for threading steel and iron pipe.

media.alt_text: RIDGID pipe threading set in case
Pipe threading tool in use

listings.notes: Apply cutting oil before each thread. I'll show you how if you haven't threaded pipe before.

items.name: Drain Camera Inspection Kit
items.description: 30m drain inspection camera with 7-inch LCD screen, LED lights, video recording. See inside pipes before you start digging. Saves you from guessing where the blockage is.

items.name: Professional Floral Arrangement Kit
items.description: Complete floral kit: 20 crystal vases (various sizes), oasis foam blocks, floral tape, wire, cutters, ribbon collection. Perfect for weddings and events. Enough for 8-10 table centerpieces.

Collection of floral arrangement tools
Crystal vases set up for event decorating

items.name: Wedding Arch & Decoration Kit
items.description: Metal wedding arch (2.2m x 1.5m), 4 column stands, white fabric draping, LED fairy lights (10m), and silk flower garlands. Sets up in 30 minutes. Used for 50+ weddings.

media.alt_text: Wedding arch decorated with flowers and fabric
Wedding ceremony setup with arch and lights

items.name: Wagner Airless Paint Sprayer
items.description: Wagner Control Pro 250M airless paint sprayer. Covers a room in 20 minutes instead of 2 hours with a roller. Low overspray technology. Includes hose, gun, and 3 tip sizes.

Wagner paint sprayer setup with hoses
Professional painting equipment ready for work

items.name: Professional Scaffold Tower (2m Platform)
items.description: Aluminium scaffold tower, 2m working platform height (3.7m reach). Lockable wheels, adjustable legs, safety rail. Holds 150kg. Fits through standard doors. Way safer than a ladder.

items.name: Industrial Sewing Machine (Juki DDL-8700)
items.description: Juki DDL-8700 single needle lockstitch. Sews everything from silk to denim to canvas. 5500 stitches per minute. Table-mounted with servo motor (quiet). Professional grade.

media.alt_text: Industrial Juki sewing machine at workstation
Sewing machine close-up with thread
Professional sewing setup with fabric

listings.notes: Available at my workshop. Bring your own fabric and thread. I'll set the tension for your material.

items.name: Adjustable Dress Form (Size 36-46)
items.description: Professional adjustable dress form, European sizes 36-46. 12-point adjustment (bust, waist, hips, back, shoulders). Wheeled stand. Essential for fitting custom garments.

media.alt_text: Specialized e-bike on Sicilian hilltop road
E-bike display showing battery and assist level

listings.notes: Charged to 100% at pickup. Charger included for multi-day rentals. The climb to Erice is nothing on this bike. Weekly rate: EUR 120.

items.description: Canyon Spectral 125 trail mountain bike. 29er wheels, 125mm travel, SRAM GX Eagle drivetrain, dropper post. Perfect for the trails around Monte Cofano and Zingaro reserve. Helmet, gloves, and pump included.

media.alt_text: Canyon mountain bike on dirt trail
Mountain bike suspension fork and disc brakes
Trail riding through Sicilian hills

listings.notes: Check tire pressure before ride (30psi front, 32psi rear). I can suggest trails for all levels. Wash the bike if you ride in mud please.

items.name: Lyre (7-String, Tortoiseshell)
items.description: Handmade tortoiseshell lyre with gut strings. The same instrument I used to accompany 27,803 lines of epic poetry. Tuned to the Dorian mode. I'll teach you the basic scales if you ask.

items.name: Epic Poetry Masterclass -- Oral Composition
items.description: Learn to compose and perform epic poetry from memory. No writing allowed. I'll teach you formulaic composition -- the technique of building poems from reusable phrases so you can improvise for hours. Bring wine. It helps.

items.name: Poetry Writing Workshop -- Finding Your Voice
items.description: Small group poetry workshop in my garden. We write, we read aloud, we revise. I focus on lyric poetry -- short, intense, personal. Bring something you've been afraid to say. That's where the poem lives.

items.name: Herb Garden Access -- Mediterranean Herbs
items.description: Pick fresh herbs from my garden: rosemary, thyme, oregano, basil, mint, bay leaf, lavender. Poets need gardens and gardens need poets. Take what you need, leave the roots.

items.name: Geometry Tutoring -- From Axioms to Proofs
items.description: Private or small group geometry lessons. We start from five axioms and build everything. No calculators. No shortcuts. Compass, straightedge, and your mind. I've been doing this for 2,300 years and the method hasn't changed because it doesn't need to.

items.name: Compass and Straightedge Set (Brass)
items.description: Professional brass compass and straightedge. The only two tools you need to construct any geometric figure. Euclid approved.

items.name: Archimedean Screw Pump (Working Model)
items.description: Working model of my water-lifting screw. Turns by hand, lifts water uphill. Still the best irrigation device for low-tech farming. I invented this 2,300 years ago and nobody's improved on it.

items.name: Physics & Engineering Tutoring -- Levers, Pulleys, Buoyancy
items.description: Hands-on physics lessons using real machines. We'll build levers, compound pulleys, and test buoyancy in water. You'll understand why ships float, how cranes work, and how I held off the Roman navy with math and mirrors.

items.name: Block and Tackle Pulley System (4:1 Mechanical Advantage)
items.description: My compound pulley design. 4:1 mechanical advantage. Lift 400kg with 100kg of force. I once pulled an entire ship onto shore using one of these to prove a point to King Hieron. Bring your own rope.

items.name: Astrolabe (Improved Hypatia Design)
items.description: My improved astrolabe design. Measures the altitude of stars and planets, tells time, finds latitude. The GPS of antiquity. Brass construction, hand-engraved. I'll teach you to read the sky.

items.name: Philosophy & Critical Thinking Workshop
items.description: Small group discussion on logic, ethics, and how to think clearly when everyone around you is losing their minds. No dogma. No doctrine. Just questions and the courage to follow them wherever they lead. I died for this. The least you can do is show up.

items.name: Poetry & Meditation Session -- The Way of the Heart
items.description: We read poetry. We sit in silence. We spin. This is not a class -- it's a practice. Bring nothing but yourself. Leave your opinions at the door. The heart knows things the mind will never understand.

listings.notes: Free. Always free. Love doesn't invoice.

items.name: Italian Literature Tutoring -- Dante to Calvino
items.description: Read the Comedy with someone who wrote it. I'll explain the cosmology, the politics, the theology, and the jokes (yes, there are jokes). Also available: Italian language lessons through poetry. You'll learn Italian the way Italians actually feel it.

items.name: Calligraphy Set -- Medieval Italian Script
items.description: Quills, iron gall ink, parchment. The tools I used to write 14,233 lines by hand. I'll show you how to hold the quill and form the letters. Your handwriting will never be the same.

items.name: Marble Sculpting Tools (Professional Set)
items.description: Point chisel, tooth chisel, flat chisel, claw chisel, rasps, rifflers. The same tool types I used on the David. Carbide-tipped for modern marble. I'll show you how to find the figure hiding in the stone.

items.name: Fresco Painting Workshop -- Wet Plaster Technique
items.description: Learn buon fresco: painting directly onto wet plaster so the pigment becomes part of the wall. You have about 8 hours before it dries. No corrections. No undo button. The technique is 3,000 years old and it's still the most permanent form of painting that exists.

items.name: Scaffolding System (Adjustable, Indoor Use)
items.description: Aluminum scaffolding tower, adjustable height up to 6 meters. Platform, guard rails, locking casters. I spent 4 years on scaffolding painting the Sistine Chapel. You probably need it for a weekend to paint your ceiling. Trust me, a ladder is not enough.

items.name: Acting Workshop -- Shakespeare for Beginners
items.description: Learn to perform Shakespeare without being terrified of the language. We start with the insults (those are the fun part), work through the monologues, and end with a scene. No experience needed. Just a voice and a willingness to look foolish -- that's how all great actors start.

items.name: Complete Works of Shakespeare (Annotated, Hardbound)
items.description: Every play, every sonnet, every poem. Annotated with historical context, word definitions, and stage directions. 1,200 pages. This is the entire human condition bound in leather. Borrow it, read one play, return it. Or keep it for a month and read them all.

items.name: Dramatic Lighting Workshop -- Chiaroscuro for Photographers
items.description: One light source. Complete darkness. That's all you need for the most dramatic images in the world. I'll teach you to see light the way I painted it -- as a weapon. Works for painters, photographers, and filmmakers. Bring your camera or your canvas.

items.name: Oil Paint Set -- Old Masters Palette (Earth Tones)
items.description: Lead white, yellow ochre, raw umber, burnt sienna, vermillion, bone black. The exact palette I used. Six colors. That's enough to paint anything if you understand value. Modern cadmium substitutes included because lead will kill you.

items.name: Etching Press (Tabletop, Adjustable Pressure)
items.description: Tabletop intaglio press for etching, drypoint, and aquatint. Adjustable pressure, steel rollers. I produced 300 etchings on a press like this. Print editions of your drawings, or learn the process from scratch.

items.name: Portrait Painting Lesson -- Light, Shadow, and Soul
items.description: I'll teach you to paint a face that looks like a person, not a photograph. We work in oil on canvas, natural light from one window. The secret isn't technique -- it's empathy. You have to love the face you're painting, even the ugly ones. Especially the ugly ones.

items.name: Harpsichord (Double Manual, Concert Quality)
items.description: Franco-Flemish double-manual harpsichord. Two 8-foot registers, one 4-foot. Proper temperament for Baroque repertoire. I wrote the Well-Tempered Clavier to prove every key was playable. This instrument proves it. Tuning included -- I'll come tune it at your venue.

items.name: Counterpoint & Fugue Composition Lesson
items.description: Learn to write a fugue. Two voices, then three, then four. Each voice independent, all voices together forming something greater than the sum. This is not just music theory -- it's how to think in parallel. Programmers love it. Every thread independent, every thread synchronized.

items.name: Fortepiano (Walter & Sohn Style Replica)
items.description: Replica of my Walter fortepiano -- the instrument I actually played, not a modern Steinway. Lighter action, clearer tone, closer to what I heard when I composed. Perfect for performing Mozart, Haydn, early Beethoven the way it was meant to sound.

items.name: Music Composition Masterclass -- From Melody to Symphony
items.description: I'll teach you what my father taught me: start with a melody that a child can hum. Then build. Harmony, counterpoint, orchestration. A symphony is just a melody that grew up. No prior theory needed -- if you can sing, you can compose.

items.name: Piano Lesson -- Beethoven Sonatas (All Levels)
items.description: Learn to play my sonatas. Moonlight, Pathetique, Appassionata, Waldstein, Hammerklavier. We start with the ones you can handle and work up to the ones that break you. The Hammerklavier has destroyed better pianists than you. That's what makes it worth trying.

items.name: Ear Trumpets (Collection of 4, Historical Replicas)
items.description: Four hearing devices from different periods of my deafness, faithfully reproduced. From the early brass cone to the elaborate conversation devices Johann Maelzel built for me. A sobering reminder that the most famous music in history was composed in silence.

items.name: Plein Air Painting Kit -- Portable Easel + Supplies
items.description: French box easel, oil paints (18 colors including chrome yellow -- my obsession), brushes, turpentine, linseed oil, and 6 pre-stretched canvases. Everything you need to paint outdoors the way the Impressionists did. Go to a wheat field. Set up. Paint what you feel, not what you see.

items.name: Expressive Painting Workshop -- Color as Emotion
items.description: I don't teach technique. I teach seeing. We go outside, we look at the sky, and I show you that it's not blue -- it's cobalt and ultramarine and violet and green, swirling. The color is the feeling. The brushstroke is the heartbeat. If your painting looks like a photograph, you're not painting -- you're copying.

items.name: Self-Portrait Workshop -- Painting Your Truth
items.description: Forget flattering yourself. Paint what's real. The scars, the joy, the fury, the love. We work in oil or acrylic on small canvases. I'll teach you about color symbolism from Mexican folk art -- what red means, what blue means, why I put monkeys and parrots in my paintings. Bring a mirror. Bring honesty.

items.name: Mexican Folk Art Supplies -- Retablo Painting Kit
items.description: Small tin sheets, oil paints, fine brushes. Everything for painting retablos -- the small votive paintings I collected and was inspired by. Paint your miracle, your gratitude, your survival. The format is tiny. The feeling is enormous.

items.name: Multimeter (Fluke 87V Industrial)
items.description: The same model I'd use if I were debugging hardware today. Measures voltage, current, resistance, capacitance, frequency, temperature. If you can measure it, you can fix it. If you can't measure it, you're guessing.

items.name: Debugging Masterclass -- Systematic Fault Isolation
items.description: I'll teach you to debug anything -- hardware, software, processes, organizations. The method is the same: isolate, measure, hypothesize, test. Stop guessing. Start measuring. And tape the bug into the logbook when you find it.

items.name: Nanosecond Wire (11.8 inches) -- Teaching Aid
items.description: A piece of wire exactly 11.8 inches long. The distance light travels in one billionth of a second. I hand these out at lectures. Hold it. Feel how short a nanosecond is. Now multiply by a billion. That's one second. Never waste a nanosecond.

media.alt_text: Circuit board close-up - teaching electronics
Science teaching materials and equipment
STEM education hands-on learning

items.name: Enigma Machine Replica (3-Rotor, Functional)
items.description: Working replica of the Wehrmacht 3-rotor Enigma. 159 quintillion possible settings. Type a letter, the rotors turn, the lampboard lights up the encrypted letter. I broke it with logic, not brute force. Try to crack a message yourself -- I'll give you hints.

items.name: Cryptography & Logic Workshop
items.description: Learn the fundamentals of code-breaking. Caesar ciphers, substitution ciphers, frequency analysis, and the principles behind Enigma. No math degree required -- just patience and the willingness to think backwards.

items.name: Running Shoes (Marathon Training)
items.description: Good trail runners. I nearly qualified for the 1948 Olympics with a 2:46 marathon. Running clears the mind better than any computer. Take them for a long run and think about impossible problems.

items.name: Jacquard Loom Cards (Sample Set + Reader)
items.description: A set of Jacquard punch cards and a demonstration reader. These are the ancestors of computer programs -- patterns encoded in holes. The loom doesn't think. It follows the card. The card is the algorithm. Babbage borrowed the idea. I wrote the first algorithm for it.

items.name: Algorithm Design Workshop -- Think Like Ada
items.description: Learn to decompose problems into repeatable steps. We start with simple sequences, add loops, add conditionals, and build up to something that could compute Bernoulli numbers -- the algorithm I wrote in 1843. Paper and pencil only. No computers. The machine is not the point. The pattern is the point.

items.name: Brass Gears & Mechanical Parts (Assorted Box)
items.description: A box of precision brass gears, cams, shafts, and springs. The same components I designed for the Analytical Engine. Build a clock, a music box, or a mechanical calculator. Or redesign them three times and never finish. Either way, the gears are beautiful.

items.name: Lock Picking Practice Set (Progressive)
items.description: 6 practice locks from simple to complex. I was fascinated by locks -- the engineering is beautiful. Pin tumbler, wafer, disc detainer. Legal to own, useful to understand, excellent for developing mechanical intuition.

items.name: Artist's Anatomy Sketchbook -- Da Vinci Method
items.description: A reproduction of my anatomical drawings with blank pages interleaved for your own studies. I dissected 30 corpses to understand how the body works. You can study from the drawings instead. Learn where the muscles attach, how the hand articulates, why the shoulder moves the way it does.

items.name: Technical Drawing & Perspective Workshop
items.description: I'll teach you linear perspective, isometric projection, and how to draw machines that could actually be built. Bring a ruler, compass, and pencils. We draw by hand. No CAD. If you can't draw it, you don't understand it.

items.name: Astrolabe (Brass, Arabic Inscriptions)
items.description: Handmade brass astrolabe with Arabic inscriptions. Navigate by the stars, find Mecca, tell time, predict sunrise and sunset. I mapped the known world. This device was my GPS. I'll teach you to read it.

items.name: Mathematics Tutoring -- From Zero to Algebra
items.description: I gave Europe the number zero. I wrote the book on algebra (al-jabr). I'll teach you both. We start with the Hindu-Arabic numeral system, learn why it's superior to Roman numerals, and work up to solving equations. The algorithm is just a recipe. Follow the steps.

items.name: Unicycle (20-inch, Learner-Friendly)
items.description: I rode this through the halls of Bell Labs while juggling. Now you can ride it through your neighborhood. 20-inch wheel, good for beginners. Balance is information theory in action -- constant feedback, constant correction.

items.name: Electronics & Boolean Logic Workshop
items.description: I'll teach you what I proved in my master's thesis: switches are logic. AND, OR, NOT. Wire them together and you can compute anything. We build circuits on a breadboard. By the end, you'll have a working binary adder made of transistors. From there, it's just scale.

items.name: The C Programming Language (K&R, 2nd Edition)
items.description: The book I co-wrote with Brian Kernighan. 272 pages. Every C programmer alive has read it. Clear, concise, no padding. The entire language specification fits in your back pocket. Borrow it, read it, write hello world, return it.

items.name: Chess Computer (Belle Replica Board)
items.description: A dedicated chess machine inspired by Belle, the first computer to earn a master rating. Play against it. It will beat you. That's the point. Learn from losing.

items.name: Scuba Diving Equipment (Full BCD + Reg Set)
items.description: BCD, regulator, octopus, pressure gauge, dive computer. I dive to get away from mailing lists. You should dive to get away from whatever is making you angry. Fits medium-large builds. Wetsuit not included.

items.name: Git & Version Control Workshop
items.description: I wrote Git in two weeks because I was angry. I'll teach it to you in two hours because you need it. Branching, merging, rebasing, conflict resolution. The fundamentals that 90% of developers still don't understand.

items.name: Web Development Basics -- HTML, HTTP, URLs
items.description: I invented the web. I'll teach you to use it. We start with a plain HTML file, no frameworks, no build tools. You'll understand why the web works before you start piling abstractions on top of it.

items.name: Homemade Wine (2024 Vintage, Red Blend)
items.description: My amateur red blend. It's not great. It's decent. The internet is better. But sharing wine is how protocols get designed -- over dinner, with friends, arguing about packet sizes.

items.name: Networking Fundamentals Workshop -- How the Internet Actually Works
items.description: Packets, routing, TCP handshakes, DNS resolution. I designed TCP/IP and I'll explain it with diagrams and a glass of wine. You'll never look at a loading spinner the same way again.

items.name: Game Theory Workshop -- Strategy, Bluffing, and Optimal Play
items.description: I co-invented game theory. I'll teach you the fundamentals: zero-sum games, Nash equilibrium, minimax strategy. We play actual games -- poker, chess, negotiation scenarios. By the end you'll understand why rational actors still make irrational decisions.

items.name: Pascaline Calculator (Working Brass Replica)
items.description: Working replica of my mechanical calculator. Six wheels. Automatic carry. Addition and subtraction. I built the original at 19 for my father. Turn the wheels and watch mathematics happen through pure mechanism.

items.name: Probability & Decision Making Workshop
items.description: Fermat and I invented probability theory by analyzing gambling. I'll teach you expected value, risk assessment, and Pascal's Wager. You'll leave understanding why the house always wins and why you should still play.

items.name: Gibson SG Custom (Electric Guitar)
items.description: The same guitar model I played at the Cotton Club in 1938. Before Chuck. Before Elvis. Before Hendrix. This is where rock and roll started -- a Black woman with a Gibson and a gospel shout.

items.name: Gospel Guitar Workshop -- Where Rock Was Born
items.description: I'll teach you the gospel guitar techniques that became rock and roll. Palm muting, string bending, distortion with feel. Church music with fire. Bring your guitar and your voice.

items.name: Gibson ES-350T (Semi-Hollow Electric Guitar)
items.description: The guitar that played Johnny B. Goode. Semi-hollow body, warm tone, cuts through any band. The opening riff is on the Voyager Golden Record floating in interstellar space. This guitar model made it happen.

items.name: Rock Guitar Riff Workshop -- The Foundations
items.description: I invented the rock guitar riff. I'll teach you the first ten. By the end of the session you'll play Johnny B. Goode, Roll Over Beethoven, and Rock and Roll Music. These are the DNA of every rock song that followed.

items.name: Piano Performance Workshop -- ATTACK the Keys
items.description: I don't teach you to play pretty. I teach you to play LOUD. Standing up. Kicking the bench. One foot on the keys. The piano is not a delicate instrument -- it's a 900-pound percussion weapon and you need to HIT IT. A-WOP-BOP-A-LOO-BOP!

items.name: New Orleans Piano Lesson -- Boogie-Woogie & Blues
items.description: The New Orleans piano style: rolling left hand, triplet feel, second-line rhythm. I'll teach you Blueberry Hill and Ain't That a Shame. Creole cooking playlist included because you can't play this music hungry.

items.name: Slide Guitar (Bottleneck Glass + Steel)
items.description: One glass slide, one steel slide. The tools that turned Delta blues into electric Chicago blues. I'll show you open tuning, slide technique, and how to make a guitar cry. The blues isn't about being sad. It's about surviving.

items.name: Electric Blues Guitar Workshop -- Chicago Style
items.description: Plug in, turn up, and play the blues. I took the Delta acoustic sound and wired it to an amplifier. The Rolling Stones named themselves after my song. Let me show you why.

items.name: Vocal Coaching -- Finding Your Voice (Rock, Gospel, Country)
items.description: I started singing gospel in church. I ended singing everything. I'll help you find your voice -- not my voice, YOUR voice. We work on breath control, vibrato, and the thing nobody teaches: vulnerability. If you're not scared when you sing, you're not singing hard enough.

items.name: Fender Stratocaster (Sunburst, Maple Neck)
items.description: The Strat. The guitar that defined rock songwriting. Three pickups, tremolo bar, the cleanest tone in rock. I played one on the Ed Sullivan Show. Plug it in, write your song, return it. Or keep writing -- that's what I would do.

items.name: Songwriting 101 -- Write Your Own Songs
items.description: The Beatles named themselves after my band. That's because I wrote my own songs -- at a time when nobody did. I'll teach you song structure: verse, chorus, bridge. Three chords and the truth. Write something today that didn't exist yesterday.

items.name: Upright Piano (Honky-Tonk, Tuned to Party)
items.description: A beat-up upright that sounds like Saturday night in Louisiana. Not a concert instrument. A PARTY instrument. Great Balls of Fire was never played on a Steinway. It was played on a piano like this, with the sustain pedal stuck and the lid wide open.

items.name: Cigar Box Guitar (Handmade, 3-String)
items.description: Built it myself. Cigar box body, broomstick neck, three strings. This is how I started. This is how rock started. You don't need a Gibson to make music. You need a box, a stick, and a beat.

items.name: Custom Guitar Building Workshop
items.description: I'll teach you to build a guitar from scratch. Cigar box, plywood, whatever you've got. Electronics, pickups, wiring. The rectangular guitar I played on stage? Built it in my garage. You can build yours in mine.

items.name: Piano & Vocal Arrangement Lesson
items.description: I arrange entire orchestras in my head, note by note. I can't see the sheet music -- I hear it. I'll teach you to arrange a song for voice and piano, then add horn lines, strings, backup vocals. Start simple. Build. Every arrangement is architecture.

items.name: Fender Stratocaster (White, Left-Handed Strung Right)
items.description: A right-handed Strat strung upside down for left-hand playing. The reversed string tension changes everything -- the tone, the bending, the harmonics. This is why I sounded different from everyone. It's not magic. It's physics and stubbornness.

items.name: Effects Pedals Masterclass -- Wah, Fuzz, Feedback
items.description: Wah-wah, fuzz face, Uni-Vibe, Octavia. I turned noise into music. I'll show you how each pedal works, how to chain them, and how to use feedback as an instrument. The Star-Spangled Banner at Woodstock was feedback, distortion, and the sound of a nation tearing itself apart.

items.name: Piano Masterclass -- Chopin Technique
items.description: Private piano lessons focusing on touch, pedaling, and rubato. I don't teach you to play loud. I teach you to play with feeling. Bring your worst piece -- we'll find the music hiding inside it.

items.name: Pleyel Upright Piano (1840 Replica)
items.description: Replica of the Pleyel pianos I played in Paris. Lighter action than modern grands. The tone is intimate, almost whispering. Perfect for nocturnes. Tuned to A=430Hz (period pitch).

items.name: Orchestration Workshop -- Scoring for Ballet
items.description: Learn to write music that makes bodies move. I'll teach you orchestral color -- how a celesta sounds like falling snow, how a bassoon sounds like a grandfather clock. We score a dance scene from scratch.

items.name: Plein Air Painting Kit (French Easel + Oils)
items.description: Portable French easel, oil paints (Winsor & Newton), brushes, canvas boards. Everything you need to paint outdoors. I spent sixty years painting outside. The light changes every five minutes. That's not a problem. That's the point.

items.name: Garden Design Consultation -- Creating a Painting Garden
items.description: I'll help you design a garden that's beautiful to paint, not just beautiful to look at. Color masses, reflection pools, arched bridges, seasonal bloom sequences. A garden is a canvas that grows.

items.name: Writing Workshop -- Finding Your Voice Through Humor
items.description: The human race has one really effective weapon, and that is laughter. I'll teach you to use it. We'll work on voice, timing, and the art of saying true things that make people laugh instead of cry. Bring something you've written. I'll be honest. Honest is all I know how to be.

items.name: Storytelling Masterclass -- The Serial Cliffhanger
items.description: Learn to structure stories that people can't stop reading. We work on chapter endings, pacing, character reveals, and the art of making your audience come back next week. I wrote A Christmas Carol in six weeks and it changed Western culture. Speed is not the enemy of quality.

items.name: Epic Novel Writing Workshop -- Structure for 500+ Pages
items.description: How to write a book that contains an entire world. We'll study structure, character management (I juggled 580 characters in War and Peace), and the art of the long form. This is not for short-attention spans. This is for people who want to build cathedrals.

items.name: Fencing Lessons -- Classical French Swordsmanship
items.description: My father was the greatest swordsman in France. I learned enough to write convincing duels. I'll teach you the basics -- en garde, parry, riposte -- and the storytelling behind every fight scene. A duel is a conversation with sharps.

items.name: Poetry Workshop -- Compression and White Space
items.description: Learn to say everything in four lines. We work on compression -- removing every word that isn't earning its place. Dashes are welcome. Capital Letters too. The goal is a poem so concentrated it detonates on contact.

items.name: Tesla Coil Kit (Educational, Low Power)
items.description: Build your own Tesla coil. Educational kit with all components, safety instructions, and my original circuit diagrams. When you see lightning jump between your hands and the coil, you'll understand why I couldn't stop. Low power -- it tingles, it doesn't kill.

items.name: Electrical Engineering Tutoring -- AC vs DC
items.description: I'll explain why alternating current won the War of Currents and why Edison was wrong. We cover electromagnetic induction, rotating magnetic fields, and power transmission. No prior knowledge needed -- just curiosity about why the lights come on.

items.name: Cubism Workshop -- See All Sides at Once
items.description: I'll teach you to draw a face from the front and side simultaneously. We'll break objects into geometric planes, reassemble them, and create something the camera can never capture. Cubism isn't abstract -- it's more real than reality. It shows everything at once.

items.name: Ceramics Studio Access -- Wheel + Kiln
items.description: Potter's wheel, electric kiln, glazes, clay. I made over 4,000 ceramic pieces in Vallauris. Come shape something with your hands. The clay doesn't care about your reputation.

items.name: Gold Leaf Application Workshop -- Klimt Technique
items.description: Learn to apply real gold leaf to paintings, furniture, or frames. I'll teach you the sizing, the breathing technique (one sneeze and twenty euros of gold blows away), and how to mix gold with oil paint for the effect you see in The Kiss. Materials included.

items.name: Physical Comedy Workshop -- Tell a Story Without Words
items.description: I made the whole world laugh without saying a word. I'll teach you timing, the pratfall, the slow burn, the double take. Your body is the instrument. No props needed -- just your willingness to fall down and get back up. That's comedy. That's life.

items.name: Large-Format Flower Painting Workshop
items.description: We paint flowers big enough that people have to stop and look. Six-foot canvases. Close-up. No background. Just the flower filling your entire field of vision. Bring your own flowers -- the ones from your garden, not the florist. Real ones.

items.name: Rhythm Workshop -- Complex Time Signatures
items.description: Learn to count in 7/8, 5/4, 11/8, and shifting meters. I'll teach you the rhythmic language of The Rite of Spring -- the piece that started a riot. Bring a drum, a table, or just your hands. Rhythm is the most primal element of music.

items.name: Trumpet Lesson -- Finding Your Sound
items.description: I don't teach you to play like me. I teach you to play like YOU. We work on embouchure, breathing, and most importantly -- phrasing. Phrasing is how you tell a story with notes. Anyone can play the right notes. The question is: can you make them mean something?

items.name: Big Band Arrangement Workshop
items.description: Learn to write for a big band -- fifteen musicians, each with a voice. I'll teach you voicings, sectional writing, and how to make an orchestra swing. The secret: write for people, not instruments.

items.name: Vocal Phrasing Workshop -- Singing Behind the Beat
items.description: I'll teach you to sing behind the beat -- to take a melody and make it yours by changing the timing, not the notes. We work on breath, emotion, and the courage to be vulnerable in front of strangers. No sheet music. Just feeling.

items.name: Modal Jazz Workshop -- Less is Everything
items.description: Kind of Blue used two scales for the entire album. Two. I'll teach you to improvise using modes instead of chord changes. Freedom through limitation. When you only have two colors, you learn to paint everything.

items.name: Piano Lesson -- Classical Technique Meets Soul
items.description: I trained for classical concert piano and ended up playing jazz in bars. That's not a step down -- that's a synthesis. I'll teach you to bring classical discipline to any genre. Bach and the blues are closer than you think.

items.name: Scat Singing Workshop -- Voice as Instrument
items.description: Learn to improvise with your voice using syllables, rhythms, and melodic invention. No words. Just sound. I'll teach you the vocabulary of scat -- the bops, the doos, the ba-da-ba-das -- and then we throw the vocabulary away and you find your own.

items.name: Songwriting Workshop -- Words First, Music Second
items.description: I write the words first. Always. The melody finds its way to the words. We'll work on imagery, metaphor, and the art of saying something so specific it becomes universal. Bring a notebook. Leave your guitar at home for the first session.

items.name: Gospel Vocal Technique Workshop
items.description: The church is where it all starts. I'll teach you to sing from the diaphragm, to bend notes, to build from a whisper to a scream that fills the room. Gospel technique is the foundation of all American popular music. If you can sing gospel, you can sing anything.

items.name: Reggae Guitar Workshop -- The Offbeat Skank
items.description: The strum that changed the world hits on the upbeat. That's it. That's the secret. But making it groove -- that's what we'll work on. I'll teach you the skank, the one-drop rhythm, and how to make three chords feel like freedom.

items.name: Persona Creation Workshop -- Inventing Your Stage Self
items.description: I'll teach you to create a character you can perform as -- costume, voice, attitude, mythology. Then we'll retire that character and build another one. The point isn't the mask. The point is the freedom the mask gives you to be truthful.

items.name: Writing Workshop -- The Sentence as Architecture
items.description: We will take your sentences apart and rebuild them. A sentence is not just communication. It is music, rhythm, architecture. Every word earns its place or it leaves. I edited for twenty years before I published. Bring your best paragraph. We'll find out if it's really your best.

items.name: Magical Realism Writing Workshop
items.description: Write the impossible as if it were the most natural thing in the world. That's the technique. No winking at the reader. No 'this is strange.' Just: 'It rained for four years, eleven months, and two days.' Stated as fact. Because in Macondo, it is. Bring a family story that nobody believes. We'll turn it into literature.

items.name: Memoir Writing Workshop -- Your Story Matters
items.description: Everyone has a story that needs telling. I went mute for five years and then I couldn't stop talking. We'll work on memory, honesty, and the courage to write the parts that scare you. Those are the parts that matter. Bring nothing but yourself and something to write with.

items.name: Songwriting Workshop -- Eighty Drafts of Hallelujah
items.description: I'll teach you to rewrite. And rewrite. And rewrite. Hallelujah took eighty drafts. That's not failure. That's craft. We start with a feeling, turn it into an image, then carve it into a verse. Bring patience. This is a slow art.

items.name: Mexican Folk Art Tour & Workshop
items.description: Visit my collection of retablos, ex-votos, and Judas figures. Then paint your own retablo -- a small tin painting of a miracle or survival. In Mexico, we thank the saints by painting what they saved us from. What has life saved you from? Paint that.

items.name: General Repair Service -- If It's Broken, Bring It
items.description: Plumbing, electrical, mechanical, whatever. I've been fixing things since I could hold a wrench. Albert Kenel taught me: don't stare at it, fix it. Bring the broken thing. I'll tell you what's wrong in thirty seconds and fix it in thirty minutes.

items.name: Complete Tool Kit (Metric + Imperial)
items.description: Full socket set, wrenches, screwdrivers, pliers, voltage tester, pipe wrench, hacksaw. Metric AND imperial because we're Swiss-Canadian and we work in both worlds. Everything in its place. Return it the same way.

items.name: Mini Excavator Rental (3.5 Ton)
items.description: Kubota KX91-3, 3.5 ton mini excavator. Rubber tracks, extendable arm, multiple bucket sizes. Perfect for residential foundations, pool digs, drainage work. I'll deliver it and show you the controls. Break something? Call me. I'll fix it.

items.name: Demolition & Site Prep Service
items.description: Small structure demolition, concrete breaking, site clearing, grading. Ideal Excavating. We break it down clean and leave the site ready to build. No mess, no shortcuts. Albert Kenel's son doesn't leave a messy jobsite.

items.name: Golf Lesson -- Fundamentals for Beginners
items.description: Grip, stance, swing, follow through. I don't teach you to hit it far. I teach you to hit it straight. Consistency beats distance every time. Just like Albert Kenel said: do it right or don't do it. Clubs provided.

items.name: Golf Club Set (Complete, Right-Handed)
items.description: Full set: driver, 3-wood, 5-wood, 4-PW irons, sand wedge, putter. Bag with stand included. Good enough to play 18 holes, not so expensive you cry when you shank one into the lake. Return them clean.

items.name: Essay Writing Workshop -- Truth as a Weapon
items.description: The essay is the most dangerous literary form because it requires you to think clearly and say what you mean. No hiding behind characters. No hiding behind metaphor. Just you and the truth and the page. I'll teach you to write sentences that change the temperature in the room.

items.name: Open Tuning Guitar Workshop -- Beyond Standard
items.description: I play in fifty different tunings because standard tuning bored me. I'll teach you DADGAD, open D, open C, and my own tunings that don't have names. Different tunings make the guitar a different instrument. One guitar, fifty voices.

items.name: Short Fiction Workshop -- Labyrinths of the Mind
items.description: We write stories that are puzzles. Stories where the form IS the content. A detective story that is also a metaphysical treatise. A book review of a book that doesn't exist. I'll teach you to write fiction that makes the reader think they are dreaming while fully awake.

items.name: Afrobeat Rhythm Workshop -- 45 Minutes of Groove
items.description: Afrobeat songs last forty-five minutes because that's how long it takes to reach transcendence. I'll teach you the interlocking rhythms -- drums, percussion, bass, guitar, horns -- that create the groove. Everyone plays a pattern. Nobody plays the whole thing. Together, you are unstoppable.

items.name: Stencil Making Workshop -- Your Message on Any Wall
items.description: I'll teach you to cut stencils, mix paint ratios, and get your message up in under sixty seconds. The art is in the preparation -- the execution takes less time than a traffic light. We practice on canvas first. What you do after that is your business. I never saw you. You were never here.

listings.notes: Free. Always. If someone charges you for Banksy lessons, it's not Banksy.

items.name: Professional Stencil Kit (Multi-Layer)
